summary: Cabrinha's high-performance freeride/big air kite with explosive boost and great hangtime. Available in standard and Aluula versions for maximum weight savings and performance.

sources.summary: Riders love the explosive pop and hangtime. The Aluula version is praised as a game-changer for light wind big air.
The Moto sits nicely between an all-around kite and a dedicated big air machine. Aluula version worth the premium.
Highlighted as one of the best performing kites in its class with fantastic boost and a fun ride feel.

reviews.video_title: Big Air Kite Review 17 - Cabrinha Moto X Design Works

reviews.verdict: demo gear to test so even if you see a kite mentioned in the comments firing an additional comment really helps me get more reviews out there finally be sure to keep up with my testing and other session updates on Instagram and as always a huge thanks for getting this far and supporting this channel this year as it has grown so please once more hit a like And subscribe if you haven't already and fire me a tip on my give a little if you're feeling generous until next time Merry Christmas [Music]
reviews.full_transcript: hello and welcome once again to plus-sized kite reviews where today I'll be focusing on the first big a focused alula release from cabrina the Moto X design works if you haven't already seen my review on the cabrina Nitro or Nitro V2 you should certainly check out the V2 review as these two kites have the same talented designer and Pat Goodman so I will reference that video a bit as well in quick summary the Nitro is still one of my favorite dakron Kites and what I think is already one of the better bigger progression Kites on the market particularly for getting into and progressing with kyops I will also be comparing this model to the recent alula kites out flown the NXT Vortex Pace Rebel DLB with perhaps a mention of the Harlem Thrive so if you would really like to go deep check out those reviews as well thanks to both cabrina and as always Mac kiteboarding for making this review possible if you want to get one of these for yourself there is a link in the description for their website but don't forget to also check out their review on it which is particularly useful because they discuss the kite in a larger size than I wrode if you're feeling generous and want to contribute to these reviews you can check out my give a little page there's also a link in the description now let's get right onto the kite having ridden a Lula kites for over a year now I can safely say that I'm quite used to the feel of them so getting onto this kite wasn't of course as huge a shock to the system as it might be for other Riders trying their first alula kite but it did have some additional unique characteristics that certainly did take some getting used to like the majority of other alula kites I have been testing lately on first impression it as you would expect quite light and responsive Ive with the general bar pressure being what I might call light to medium for alula similar in general pressure to the slingshot NXT which probably makes it a little more than the ozone vortex in medium strength wind uh but a little lighter than say the core Pace unlike the vortex however it doesn't seem to increase as dramatically as the wind picks up over 30 knots the general steering and movement of the kite for the most part feels similar to a Nitro or orbit just faster of course and the bar does a lovely job of telling you where the kite is at all times and what the kite is doing as you fly it through the window I was able to ride the Moto on a few different sessions now with slightly different conditions varing from as little as 20 to 25 knots to some later 20s averages topping just over 30 knots in general I found the kite the most enjoyable when the wind was consistent and mod moderately power power so around the 25 to 30 knot region alth the co although the kite had quite a good low end for a 9 M as you'd expect at my size it still wasn't enough to do a lot with it in the low 20s and when the wind got over 30 some of the characteristics I'll mention um got a little bit more challenging to control which again is not uncommon for a super fast high performance alula Big Air kite if you have heard me talk about uh my original Evo daab reviews and some of the other ones like the pace for example this kite certainly has the majority of the elements you are looking for in an alula kite in that it moves very fast and has the tendency to ride further forward in the window and when sending the kite for jumps with good timing it will shoot quickly in the directions traveling to give you a great yank off the water for your drums it also similar to the NXT has a tend see to surprise you with just how much lift and hang time you'll get out of it whenever you manage to harness it potential but I would say that it can be a bit more challenging to time your boosts as the kite is super fast and at other times try to figure out how to make it fly the most consistently um to make the most of all of these features while we were getting used to the kite there were times when the kite felt a bit unbalanced in terms of its power deliver dely while in stronger but less consistent or Gusty winds and it seems to have a slight tendency on occasion to not like being fully sheeted in and you'd find yourself losing some power as you move the kite while holding down or just pulling too much on the bar a friend of mine who was also testing the kite with me and is at my level described it like this it's a light kite that needs to be ridden light so if you bury your Edge or over sheet when it's not moving quickly it will let you know it's not happy with a bit of a back stall so you have to let it breathe and work it around and it will remain very Lively and have plenty of power and pinpoint steering of course this could all be our Rider error but we both often found we were trying not to pull the bar all the way in as we mooved the kite around the window and by doing so seemed to stay more consistently powered when the wind got Gusty as we are only testing the 9 M size it's of course impossible for us to say if this would be as much of an issue in other sizes but my guess is that it wouldn't be for larger size kites as you are unlikely to be riding them as powered and trying to go for Big Air specific riding um if you check out the Mac boys riding uh with the 12 M size they didn't seem to find this happening for them at all the kite's test rider steno also seems to perform the best with the 8 m size in particular and I've heard in the kit surf 365 podcast the boys talk about that being his favorite size in the quiver now for the most part you can also take our critique with a grain of salt as we seem to get used to how the kitee moved and the correct pressure to apply to the bar after about an hour or so on it and it was really a major factor when the conditions were good as I've also mentioned in the reviews for some of the other alula kites like the orbit Ultra or the core Pace some of the characteristics of these high performance alula models do seem to either be targeted at a much higher level Rider or perhaps even more specifically targeted or designed for the pro team Rider who helped with the design of the kite and the testing so someone at our level or below will just take a bit more getting used to it or likely not even get what they're looking for in a kite with all that said let's take a look at the various categories I judge the Big Air kites I ride on starting with boosting as I mentioned above there is a lot of potential here with this kite but it can be a tricky one to get the most out of for me it didn't have the immediately easy sheet and go that I've gotten a little used to recently trying the rebels or even that I've found with my Evo over time but it certainly felt like when you did hit the timing right with it there was a whole lot of lift yanking you off the water I didn't have any serious sessions where I was really jumping super high all of my sessions topping out around the 15 to 18 Mark and as I tend to do a lot of super fast kites I was over sending it a lot of the time trying to hit the sweet spot I had one day where I went from the rebel SLS to this Moto then to the Nitro and I was um and I found I was jumping slightly higher on the rebel with the Moto a little bit behind that and then the Nitro quite far behind them both so to me the ula's addition of the stiffness the thinner Leading Edge and its General design is certainly doing what it's supposed to here with that info if I do a little kite math for my level that would put the Moto for me somewhere in the range of kites like the ultra or the pace just a shade behind the likes of the vortex and a tier below the higher jumping kites like the NXT Rebels evos now with that said I do believe it has more potential lift than I was able to get out of it in the shorter sessions that I had and with more time on it I think I could get a lot higher in comparison to some of the other kites it's just hard to say at this stage in terms of the hang time I did find this a bit more easily accessible because on so many of my jumps i' find find myself getting a little added lift or float as the kite passed overhead particularly after a nice kite loop I would get a floaty catch a lot of the time and certainly more lift than and hang time than I was Finding on say the orbits or the paces or those mid to high tier jumpers this again was rather similar in feel to the NXT even perhaps a little more hangtime than the vortex the kite was also great for hel Loops in that you could crank the bar for high and tight Loops to retain some lift or you could pull a little softer swing the kite well behind you and get a beautiful ton of lift for super soft Landings for powered kite Loops the kite is bordering on excellent were it not for one nasty habit you might come across where if you have the kite in a bad position relative to the wind likely by over sending the kite a bit out of this window uh which can be easy to do as I found with the kite speed or the wind is particularly holy then when you pull in the bar too much causing the kite to do a very tight Loop where it almost pivots on itself a bit too much you seem to be able to choke it out as it gets through the loop and it can take a moment before it engages and shoots back up to 12 which can with some bad luck cause you to not get the cleanest catch and you can fall out of the sky a little bit I only actually had this happen to be a few times over the several sessions like you can see in that last clip and after a while on the kite each session with a few more Loops in the bag I felt like I had gotten the kite figured out on how to avoid it so I was starting to do some small rotations but then on my last session on the Moto I got a bit cocky and didn't put in the required time to adjust the cut and I had it happened quite early in this session as I was trying to do a small Boogie about 78 M High which resulted in this Nast clip you can see you can kind of see as I come out of the rotation where the kite just pivots on itself and stops and I fall it of the sky my friends and I felt like this was happening most when you would just crank the bar too much so that rather then do a full loop the kite seems to Loop too tightly and cause you to get some line slack after it finishes which slows down the ascent for a brief pause which at the wrong time can be just enough to drop you luckily the kite is so fast it will usually still catch you that brief pause can be enough to drop you like you can see in this clip if you're not high in the kite's defense this can be chocked up to rer as well but this is a ryer eror I don't find myself doing on other kites so this just uh adds a bit more technical difficulty to looping and progressing with loops on the Moto X I am curious if this characteristic is a result of Designing this kite to be able to be better for double Loops in the smaller sizes as I found the core Pace or Harlem Thrive also had a bit of this tendency to Loop so fast they were almost pivoting on a single point rather than always traveling around a full loop in those kites however they both always seemed to recover so quickly that there wasn't that same pause after the loop and they all rocketed back up to 12 for a cat um perhaps I'm not explaining this characteristic correctly and I would say that a more experienced rider or kite designer might be able to better explain why my friends and I were having this happen to us on occasion um and it can be just avoided with better T technique like not over sending the kite of course or trying to Loop it outside the windows what much um you certainly also with this kite have the ability to ease out the bar a little bit and get a beautiful smooth looping motion as you see in most of the clips on this video and even as you start to pile out the kite lower through your Loops the kite is super quick so the pull is not not too challenging to control for the most part so all in all still one of the absolute best looping kites out there in terms of General potential but by no means the absolute easiest to progress on like say the Nitro for example so with that said in terms of the kite's confidence boosting factor for the most part it's quite good because the kite in general feels pretty easy to use for the performance that offers but like the pace for example just takes a bit more getting getting used to knowing I didn't even appreciate my evos in the smaller sizes when I first tried them uh it reminds me this kind of performance is something you do need to adjust to in time obviously depending on your level but at least in the smaller sizes I would say this kite is more targeted for the advanced Rider already at a level where they are doing more advanced loops and Technical big air and progressing Riders might struggle with it luckily cabrina already make one of the best Big Air progression kites out there in the Nitro so if you are looking just to get into your first loops and progressing into bigger ones or basic Loop variations that is where I would probably direct you in these smaller sizes say N9 and under however from 10 m and up given this Moto's low-end power and its lightweight I would say it could be a solid option for those sizes for even progressing Riders and those looking for some multidiscipline use to help round out a very tasty cabrina quiver for me personally of the range I would take the Moto um over the Nitro in all sizes because I do like that extra power and performance and like with all the 9M Big Air kites I had a great time on I am really interested to try the 8 m in some really big winds if I ever get the chance even more so having recently seen what their team Rider stino is able to do with it in terms of height big powered loops and endless looking hand Hang Time not that we're even doing the same sport really but dreams are cheaper than kites so there you have it another brilliant kite and another review in the bag as always I'd love to hear of your experiences with this kite in the comments and given that my next video will most likely be an end of the year summary and my picks for the best kites of the year I'd really be interested to hear what are your top few kites of 2024 your comments and feedback on what kites you'd like me to be reviewing are the biggest tool I have to getting hold of new demo gear to test so even if you see a kite mentioned in the comments firing an additional comment really helps me get more reviews out there finally be sure to keep up with my testing and other session updates on Instagram and as always a huge thanks for getting this far and supporting this channel this year as it has grown so please once more hit a like And subscribe if you haven't already and fire me a tip on my give a little if you're feeling generous until next time Merry Christmas [Music]
